bugs.description: UserPublic — the schema serialized back to API clients — now declares `hashed_password`. Because the User table model has that column, every user response will leak the password hash to the client. The field looks like an innocent addition; it's only dangerous once you know UserPublic is the public response contract and hashed_password is a secret on the table.

injection.replace_with:
class UserPublic(UserBase):
    id: uuid.UUID
    created_at: datetime | None = None
    hashed_password: str

bugs.description: UserUpdateMe is the body schema for the self-service `PATCH /users/me` endpoint. Adding `is_superuser` to it lets any authenticated user promote themselves to admin by setting the flag on their own profile update — a mass-assignment privilege escalation. Catching it requires knowing which endpoint consumes this schema and that is_superuser is a privileged field, not just reading the diff.

injection.replace_with:
class UserUpdateMe(SQLModel):
    full_name: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=255)
    email: EmailStr | None = Field(default=None, max_length=255)
    is_superuser: bool = False

bugs.description: Item.owner_id drops its `ondelete="CASCADE"`, but User.items still declares `Relationship(..., cascade_delete=True)`. The two halves of the same relationship now disagree: the ORM expects deletes to cascade while the database foreign key no longer enforces it, so deleting a user with items will raise a FK-constraint error (or orphan rows). The bug is only visible by cross-referencing the User side of the relationship with the Item side.

UserUpdate lowers the password `min_length` to 6, while UserCreate (signup) still enforces 8. The password policy is now inconsistent across the two schemas: a user can weaken their password below the registration minimum on update. Detecting it means comparing the constraint in one model class against the other.

injection.find: password: str | None = Field(default=None, min_length=8, max_length=128)
injection.replace_with: password: str | None = Field(default=None, min_length=6, max_length=128)
